title: balsamina noli-tangere
intro: Balsamina noli-tangere, also known as the "Vestal Flower" or "Virgin's Bower," is a flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, with its name derived from the Latin words "balsamum" meaning "balsam" and "noli tangere" meaning "touch me not." This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Balsamina noli-tangere include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Balsamina noli-tangere is typically prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is also used as a component in various herbal remedies.
